name: Diamond Head Beach
tagline: A hidden stretch below the lighthouse cliffs that locals use for bodyboarding, tidepooling, and escaping the Waikiki scene.
parking: Street parking along Diamond Head Rd near the lighthouse; walk down the cliffside trail
bestTime: South swell season (summer) for surf; morning for tidepools
talkStory: Getting here requires a short but steep walk down from the road — which is exactly why it stays uncrowded. The bodyboarding here is legit during south swells. There are cool tide pools along the rocky edges at low tide. Not a sandy lounge-around beach; more of an adventure spot. Combine with the Diamond Head hike for a full morning. Bring water shoes — the entry is rocky.
